  • Press Release

  • ROS ALTMANN

    Ros is a leading authority on later life issues, including pensions,
    social care and retirement policy. Numerous major awards have recognised
    her work to demystify finance and make pensions work better for people.
    She was the UK Pensions Minister from 2015 – 16 and is a member
    of the House of Lords where she sits as Baroness Altmann of Tottenham.

    Category: Campaign for Pensions Justice

    ‘What can you do with £10?’ — pensioners bemused by Christmas

    ‘What can you do with £10?’ — pensioners bemused by Christmas

    December 19, 2025 Ros Altmann

    The Times quotes Ros expressing views about the tiny pensioner Christmas Bonus of £10 a year and highlights that such pensioner giveaways are not much valued, but still cost significant sums to taxpayers – either it should be increased and taxable, or changed to help the poorest.

    Open External article Read More

    Stand-off in House of Lords as Lady Altmann attacks inheritance tax and pensions red tape nightmare

    Stand-off in House of Lords as Lady Altmann attacks inheritance tax and pensions red tape nightmare

    November 19, 2025 Ros Altmann

    ThisIsMoney reports on Ros’ Oral Question in the House of Lords in which she challenges the Treasury Minister about the damaging impact that levying IHT on usused pensions will inflict on heirs, executors and future pensions.

    Open External article Read More

    Fury over suggestion state pension age could be increased to 80

    Fury over suggestion state pension age could be increased to 80

    August 5, 2025 Ros Altmann

    Ros is quoted by the Daily Express commenting on the unfairness of suggestions that the State Pension Age should be raised to anything like age 80, when so many less well-off people would never receive a penny, despite a lifetime of National Insurance contributions. ( link to pdf )

    Open External article Read More

    Keir Starmer putting pensioners ‘at bottom’ of priority list, warns former minister

    Keir Starmer putting pensioners ‘at bottom’ of priority list, warns former minister

    May 11, 2025 Ros Altmann

    Ros is quoted in the Daily Express, lamenting the low priority that Government seems to be giving to pensioners. ( link to pdf )

    Open External article Read More

    Government urged to compel 25% UK investment as ‘quid pro quo’ for tax relief

    Government urged to compel 25% UK investment as ‘quid pro quo’ for tax relief

    May 6, 2025 Ros Altmann

    Ros is quoted by Professional Pensions calling for at least 25% of new pension contributions to be invested in UK markets, in exchange for tax relief. ( link to pdf )

    Open External article Read More

    150,000 more retirees will miss out on pension credit – and winter fuel allowance

    150,000 more retirees will miss out on pension credit – and winter fuel allowance

    April 24, 2025 Ros Altmann

    Ros is quoted by inews, explaining that those pensioners with incomes slightly above the Pension Credit cut-off urgently need their Winter Fuel Payment but have lost it.

    Open External article Read More

    Spread the message there is no shame in claiming pension credit, says new campaign

    Spread the message there is no shame in claiming pension credit, says new campaign

    November 23, 2024 Ros Altmann

    ThisIsMoney explains how to claim Pension Credit and mentions Ros’ opposition to the means-testing of Winter Fuel Payments. ( link to pdf )

    “Disgrace In 21st-Century Britain” Thousands Of Pensioners Face Winter Fuel Payment Cut

    “Disgrace In 21st-Century Britain” Thousands Of Pensioners Face Winter Fuel Payment Cut

    September 30, 2024 Ros Altmann

    Ros is one of the experts chosen by The Sun newspaper to help readers understand more about eligibility for Winter Fuel Payments.

    Open External article Read More

    Winter Fuel fury as British pensioners £755 worse off than Irish after Labour raid

    Winter Fuel fury as British pensioners £755 worse off than Irish after Labour raid

    September 23, 2024 Ros Altmann

    Ros is quoted in the Daily Express explaining that the Government’s decision to take Winter Fuel Payments away from so many of the poorest pensioners contrasts very badly with the greater generosity of the Irish help for pensioners’ winter fuel costs. ( link to pdf )

    Open External article Read More

    State pension set to rise to £230 per week due to wage increases

    State pension set to rise to £230 per week due to wage increases

    September 10, 2024 Ros Altmann

    Ros is quote in inews explaining why the suggestion that the rise in State Pension by a few pounds a week from April 2025, can’t possibly offset the lost hudnreds of pounds needed for winter 2024.
